source_jobs.title: Business Development Manager, Immunoassays & Biomarkers (MidAtlantic)
source_jobs.description:
Spear Bio, founded in 2021, is a well-funded and rapidly growing biotech start-up headquartered in Woburn, Massachusetts.  Our proprietary technology, Successive Proximity Extension Amplification Reaction (SPEAR), is a novel wash-free immunoassay platform, offering unprecedented sensitivity. We are dedicated to revolutionizing early disease diagnosis and monitoring by providing scalable, ultrasensitive solutions that deliver unparalleled accuracy in measuring low-abundant biomarkers, enabling clinicians and researchers to improve patient outcomes and advance global health.

Position Summary:

Spear Bio is seeking top-performing Business Development Managers to introduce our high-performance and expanding portfolio of ultra-sensitive Immunoassays to the Life Sciences and CRO markets. We are hiring individuals based in the MidAtlantic (Philadelphia, DC, Maryland, Virginia) with a deep network of neurodegenerative disease research customers in Big Pharma, Biotech, CROs, & Academic throughout the MidAtlantic region.



Key Responsibilities:

 - Deliver sales presentations to clients to promote the value proposition of Spear Bio.

 - Demonstrate deep understanding of Neuroscience market dynamics and technology adoption which positions and drives confidence in Spear Bio customers that we are a superior, best-in-class solution.

 - Promote our kit sales by providing various instrument and Assay Service solutions to generate ongoing market adoption and revenue growth.

 - Provide a funnel to be managed by senior Business Development Managers

 - Be on-point for all customer logistics and follow up: Demos, information sharing, and other key technical details requested to engage our customers.

 - Utilize your existing network of high-level scientific customers within Big Pharma, Biotech, CROs, & Academic market segments, as well as knowledge of IVD markets including LDTs.

 - Achieve a consistent track record exceeding quotas, revenue targets, and increasing market share in your primary territory/region.

 - Provide technical support to many of our Key Opinion Leaders throughout the US/EMEA.

 - Work closely with our internal Marketing team and contribute to driving lead generation programs that can enhance our outreach in all regions.

 - Work closely with our Assay Services leader to support funnel building of this area, as well as provide Voice of Customer (VOC) feedback to our R&D teams on market dynamics gained with our commercial activity.

 - Jointly build a sales plan with personal and professional goals that will provide the best metrics for success in the role.

 - Provide regular updates on activity, forecasting, and funnel movement to assist in the transparency of activity driving strong revenue outcomes.

 - Apply insight on current competitive landscape to develop strategies enabling Spear Bio to win in a competitive market where we have a strong scientific advantage.

 - Approximately 30-40% travel expected directly with customers and/or partners to achieve the sales results expected.



Required Qualifications:

 - MS or PhD in Biology, Biochemistry, Bioengineering or related field with 3-10+ years of an established track record in sales or business development, presenting ultrasensitive immunoassays or neurology IVD products to customers.

 - Clear and pro-active communicator with advanced commercial sales acumen, high energy, a strong work ethic, and a bias for action.

 - Ability to ask the right questions of potential customers to then strategically communicate how Spear Bio's differentiated technology and performance will directly benefit them over the competition.

 - Ability to clearly present technology solutions in a way that leads to strong revenue outcomes.

 - Ability to translate observations and customer feedback into strategy.

 - Comfortable providing and receiving feedback designed to improve engagement and results.

 - Ability to collaborate within a best-in-class team environment comprised of Field Ap

source_jobs.title: Bioengineering Associate, Automation & Systems Testing.
source_jobs.description:
Spear Bio, founded in 2021, is a well-funded and rapidly growing biotech start-up headquartered in Woburn, Massachusetts.  Our proprietary technology, Successive Proximity Extension Amplification Reaction (SPEAR), is a novel wash-free immunoassay platform, offering unprecedented sensitivity. We are dedicated to revolutionizing early disease diagnosis and monitoring by providing scalable, ultrasensitive solutions that deliver unparalleled accuracy in measuring low-abundant biomarkers, enabling clinicians and researchers to improve patient outcomes and advance global health.

We are seeking a motivated BioEngineering Associate to join our Automation and Systems Engineering team. In this role, you will support the testing and validation of our Immunoassay Systems by executing systematic test strategies across assay workflows and integrated engineering systems. Your work will directly contribute to ensuring our platforms deliver reliable, reproducible, and high-quality results.

The ideal candidate is passionate about working at the intersection of biology and engineering. You will play a key role in validating performance across assay workflows and hardware/software interfaces, helping the team identify root causes of failures and ensuring Spear Bio's platforms consistently deliver high-quality results.

This role is based on-site in our lab in Woburn, MA 5 days/week.

Key Responsibilities:

Testing & Validation

 - Run and document tests on automated assay workflows and engineering subsystems.

 - Evaluate reproducibility, robustness, and sensitivity of proteomic and molecular biology assays.

 - Support system-level testing of fully automated workflows combining assay and instrumentation.

Troubleshooting

 - Help identify whether performance issues stem from assay chemistry, workflow automation, or hardware subsystems.

 - Assist in root cause analysis to resolve failures and improve system reliability.

Data & Reporting

 - Analyze and organize test data using Excel or similar tools.

 - Help generate reports and dashboards tracking assay performance and validation results.

Cross-Disciplinary Collaboration

 - Work closely with cross-functional teams across biology and engineering.

 - Participate in design discussions to ensure systems are built with testability in mind.



Qualifications:

Education & Experience

 - Bachelor’s or Master’s in Biomedical Engineering, Bioengineering, Chemical Engineering, Systems Engineering, Mechanical/Electrical Engineering, or related field.

 - 0–3 years of experience in bioengineering testing or validation of immunoassays or nucleic acid assays.

Technical Skills

 - Hands-on experience with immunoassays and/or qPCR systems.

 - Experience with liquid handling automation, mechatronic instruments, or integrated life science systems.

 - Basic programming or scripting skills (Python, C#, or similar) for test automation or data analysis.

 - Familiarity with statistical analysis and data visualization for assay or system performance.

Soft Skills:

 - Analytical mindset with a systematic approach to problem-solving

 - Strong documentation and communication skills

 - Comfortable collaborating across teams in a fast-paced environment.



We offer competitive compensation, meaningful stock ownership, comprehensive benefits, and a great work environment. This is an opportunity to enter an innovative, high-growth startup at an early stage and play an integral role in the company's growth and success. We are focused on identifying candidates who have demonstrated leadership and excellence throughout their careers and are excited by the prospect of building an industry-leading company.

Spear Bio Inc. provides equal opportunity in employment to all persons. No person shall be denied equal access because of race, creed, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, or physical/mental disability.

Spear Bio does not accept resume submissions from external recruit

source_jobs.title: Field Application Scientist (FAS)
source_jobs.description:
Spear Bio, founded in 2021, is a well-funded and rapidly growing biotech start-up headquartered in Woburn, Massachusetts.  Our proprietary technology, Successive Proximity Extension Amplification Reaction (SPEAR), is a novel wash-free immunoassay platform, offering unprecedented sensitivity. We are dedicated to revolutionizing early disease diagnosis and monitoring by providing scalable, ultrasensitive solutions that deliver unparalleled accuracy in measuring low-abundant biomarkers, enabling clinicians and researchers to improve patient outcomes and advance global health.


FIELD APPLICATION SCIENTIST

Due to Commercial growth, Spear Bio is expanding its Field Application Support Team. We are looking to hire technically strong Field Application Scientists or Technical Salespeople. These individuals should have extraordinary communication, presentation, and technical troubleshooting skills, and a strong network within RUO, pharma, and academic areas.

In this dynamic, technical, and customer-facing role, you will handle pre-sales customer visits, customer training, workflow demonstrations, customer sample testing in the lab, applications development, and technical support of our range of immunoassay solutions.

The Field Application Scientist will develop strong technical expertise in Spear Bio's ultrasensitive immunoassay platform to expertly navigate, troubleshoot, and resolve complex technical issues for customers. This role involves ~30-40% travel to customer sites throughout the U.S. (primarily East Coast), and is expected to spend time working with assays in the lab in Woburn, MA when not traveling.

 


RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Support and train external customers, internal scientists within Spear Bio, and partner organizations on SPEAR UltraDetect™ immunoassay technology and workflow.

 - Drive successful adoption of the SPEAR platform by providing technical expertise for key customers through on-site product demos, training, collaborative projects, and support via e-mail/phone.

 - Perform client sample testing for platform evaluations, generate data, and present performance results to clients.

 - Work cross-functionally to develop new applications for current products, and generate data and technical collateral including application notes and conference presentations.

 - Improve workflow processes when troubleshooting customer immunoassay challenges. Triage issues, develop hypotheses, perform experiments and investigations, and identify solutions to ensure optimal product performance.

 - Track and address customer successes, inquiries, and complaints, to provide feedback for continuous improvement of products and technical support.

 - Maintain up-to-date knowledge of competitive landscape and key product applications.

 - Work with Product Management team to provide voice-of-customer feedback for product improvements and strategies that will drive adoption of Spear Bio products.

 


REQUIREMENTS:

 - PhD or MS in Biology, Biochemistry, Neuroscience, or Bioengineering

 - 2+ years of hands-on lab and troubleshooting experience with immunoassays and instrumentation for biomarker analysis. Scientists developing 'omics' diagnostic assays also considered. Strong record of first-author publications.

 - 2+ years in-person customer pre and post-sales, demos, training, complex troubleshooting, as highly technical Field Application Scientist. Strong network within RUO, pharma, and academic areas.

 - Demonstrated resourcefulness and ability to independently implement new technologies and troubleshoot instrument or assay performance issues.

 - Experience with qPCR and liquid handling machines

 - Knowledge of neurodegenerative disease research with existing network in drug development, academic, or contract research world preferred

 - Ability to travel up to 40% (including overnights), with occasional international travel

General Qualifications:

 - Strong commitment to ensuring customer success

 - Engaging interpersonal

source_jobs.title: Scientist/Senior Scientist, Assay Development - Molecular Assay Chemistry and Biophysics
source_jobs.description:
Spear Bio, founded in 2021, is a well-funded and rapidly growing biotech start-up headquartered in Woburn, Massachusetts.  Our proprietary technology, Successive Proximity Extension Amplification Reaction (SPEAR), is a novel wash-free immunoassay platform, offering unprecedented sensitivity. We are dedicated to revolutionizing early disease diagnosis and monitoring by providing scalable, ultrasensitive solutions that deliver unparalleled accuracy in measuring low-abundant biomarkers, enabling clinicians and researchers to improve patient outcomes and advance global health.


SCIENTIST/SENIOR SCIENTIST, ASSAY DEVELOPMENT - MOLECULAR ASSAY CHEMISTRY AND BIOPHYSICS

Our Assay Development team builds assays for low-abundance protein biomarkers that are challenging to measure using conventional approaches. We are seeking a creative, hands-on scientist with strong fundamentals in chemistry, biophysics, and molecular interactions who enjoys solving difficult technical problems from first principles.

The Scientist / Senior Scientist will design, optimize, and troubleshoot innovative protein-detection assays that integrate antibody-based recognition, nucleic-acid interactions, conjugation chemistry, reaction kinetics, and quantitative analytical methods.

We are looking for a scientist who genuinely enjoys troubleshooting. The ideal candidate is persistent, analytical, creative, and comfortable working through uncertainty. This person should be motivated by understanding the underlying mechanism and should thrive in a fast-moving startup environment with high scientific standards.

The successful candidate will be comfortable investigating why an assay behaves unexpectedly, developing mechanistic hypotheses, designing discriminating experiments, and systematically improving assay sensitivity, specificity, robustness, and reproducibility.

We welcome applications from exceptional recent Ph.D. graduates and postdoctoral fellows with strong hands-on research ownership, as well as startup and industry scientists who have demonstrated rapid growth in technical scope and responsibility. **This role is based onsite in our lab in Woburn, MA 5 days/week.




RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Design and develop ultrasensitive protein-detection assays from concept through feasibility, optimization, analytical characterization, verification, and transfer.

 - Develop and optimize antibody and protein-reagent to maximize analytical and clinical performance , including antibody selection, protein structural properties, epitope-pair assessment, conjugation chemistry, reagent purification, and stability evaluation.

 - Troubleshoot assay-performance issues, including low signal, nonspecific background, limited sensitivity, poor reproducibility, matrix effects, instability, and lot-to-lot variability, using a mechanistic understanding of binding kinetics, affinity, avidity, thermodynamics, steric effects, and molecular interactions.

 - Design efficient experiments and use structured approaches, including design of experiments where appropriate, to identify root causes and optimize performance.

 - Collaborate closely with Platform, Product Development, Operations, and Business Development teams to transition assays from feasibility through verification and transfer.

 - Prepare clear scientific presentations, technical reports, study summaries, abstracts, and publications.




REQUIREMENTS:

 - Ph.D. in · Chemistry, Analytical Chemistry, Biophysics, Chemical Engineering, Biochemistry, Bioengineering, Molecular Engineering, or a closely related field.

 - Strong fundamental understanding of molecular interactions involving nucleic acids, proteins, or both.

 - Experience with protein–protein and antigen–antibody interactions, including affinity, avidity, kinetics, and thermodynamics.

 - Demonstrated hands-on experience creating and designing immunoassays, protein-detection methods, molecular-diagnostics assays, or an innovative life-science analytical platform.

source_jobs.title: Research Associate
source_jobs.description:
Spear Bio, founded in 2021, is a well-funded and rapidly growing biotech start-up headquartered in Woburn, Massachusetts.  Our proprietary technology, Successive Proximity Extension Amplification Reaction (SPEAR), is a novel wash-free immunoassay platform, offering unprecedented sensitivity. We are dedicated to revolutionizing early disease diagnosis and monitoring by providing scalable, ultrasensitive solutions that deliver unparalleled accuracy in measuring low-abundant biomarkers, enabling clinicians and researchers to improve patient outcomes and advance global health.


RESEARCH ASSOCIATE:

We are seeking an experienced and talented Research Associate to join our Assay Development team, with a strong scientific background and the desire to be at the forefront of innovative research.

 


RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Execute immunoassay workflows with a focus on high-throughput, semi-automated platforms

 - Assist in optimizing methods for processing different types of clinical samples for assay development

 - Set up, calibrate, and maintain laboratory equipment, based on the specification of various projects

 - Assist in laboratory analysis and data management

 - Acquire necessary equipment and supplies for various projects

 - Prepare materials for submission to granting agencies and foundations

 


QUALIFICATIONS:

 - BS in Biology, Biochemistry, Biomedical Engineering or a related discipline plus minimum 2-3+ years in an academic or industry lab, or MS degree with 1-2+ years of related experience.

 - Direct experience running immunoassays and qPCR experimentation is required

 - Experience characterizing and troubleshooting immunoassays

 - Strong proficiency in molecular biology lab techniques


GENERAL QUALIFICATIONS:

 - Self-motivated and ability to perform complex tasks in an effective manner

 - Ability to follow instructions, learn, and grow in a mentorship setting

 - Excellent organizational and documentation skills

 - Desire to learn about and implement new technologies

 - Strong presentation and interpersonal skills with the capability to establish collaborative working relationships and become a key contributor within the assay development, platform and product teams.

 - Work 5 days/week on site in Woburn, MA. LOCAL CANDIDATES will be given priority consideration for this position.

 



We offer competitive compensation, meaningful stock ownership, comprehensive benefits, and a great work environment. This is an opportunity to enter an innovative, high-growth startup at an early stage and play an integral role in the company's growth and success. We are focused on identifying candidates who have demonstrated leadership and excellence throughout their careers and are excited by the prospect of building an industry-leading company.

Spear Bio Inc. provides equal opportunity in employment to all persons. No person shall be denied equal access because of race, creed, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, or physical/mental disability.

Spear Bio does not accept resume submissions from external recruiting agencies. Any unsolicited resumes or candidate information submitted without prior written agreement will be deemed the property of Spear Bio, and no placement fees will be paid.

source_jobs.title: Clinical Lab Director (Part-Time)
source_jobs.description:
Spear Bio, founded in 2021, is a well-funded and rapidly growing biotech start-up headquartered in Woburn, Massachusetts.  Our proprietary technology, Successive Proximity Extension Amplification Reaction (SPEAR), is a novel wash-free immunoassay platform, offering unprecedented sensitivity. We are dedicated to revolutionizing early disease diagnosis and monitoring by providing scalable, ultrasensitive solutions that deliver unparalleled accuracy in measuring low-abundant biomarkers, enabling clinicians and researchers to improve patient outcomes and advance global health.

Spear Bio is seeking a part-time Laboratory Director to support the establishment, certification, and operation of our CLIA-certified laboratory in Massachusetts. The Laboratory Director will provide clinical, scientific, and regulatory oversight to ensure Spear Bio delivers accurate, reliable, and high-quality testing services in compliance with applicable regulatory and accreditation requirements, including CLIA, CAP, and other applicable state requirements.

The Laboratory Director will be responsible for overseeing laboratory QMS, ensuring appropriate validation and performance of immunoassay testing methods, reviewing laboratory procedures, and supporting continuous improvement initiatives. This role provides clinical and technical guidance for test interpretation, quality assurance and regulatory compliance.

This is a consulting role intended to support Spear Bio’s CLIA/CAP certification readiness and early clinical laboratory operations. The Laboratory Director may delegate specific duties to qualified personnel but retains the ultimate responsibility for ensuring these duties are properly performed and documented.



Responsibilities:

 - Serve as the CLIA Laboratory Director for Spear Bio’s high-complexity clinical laboratory.

 - Provide overall clinical, scientific, and regulatory oversight of CLIA-certified laboratory operations.

 - Ensure compliance with all applicable regulatory and accreditation requirements, including CLIA, CAP, and NY State CLEP, as applicable.

 - Oversee quality management program to ensure accurate, reliable, and timely laboratory testing.

 - Review and approve laboratory policies, procedures, test validations, and quality documentation.

 - Monitor laboratory performance and implement continuous quality improvement initiatives.

 - Provide clinical consultation to physicians and other healthcare providers regarding laboratory testing and test results.

 - Support laboratory readiness for regulatory inspections and accreditation surveys.

 - Foster a culture of quality, compliance, and scientific excellence.


Qualifications

 - M.D., D.O., or Ph.D. in a relevant scientific discipline (e.g., immunology, pathology, clinical chemistry, biochemistry, molecular biology, or a related field).

 - Qualified to serve as Clinical Laboratory Director of a high-complexity CLIA laboratory under applicable federal and Massachusetts state regulations -- Clinical Laboratory Director License issued by the Massachusetts Department of Public Health (MDPH) is required

 - Board certification in a relevant discipline (e.g., Clinical Chemistry, Clinical Pathology, HCLD, Immunology, or other appropriate specialty) is required.

 - Minimum 8-10 years of experience in high complexity clinical laboratory operations, with at least 5 years in a director-level role overseeing a CAP/CLIA-certified laboratory.

 - Proven track record of successfully establishing or scaling a clinical lab.

 - Demonstrated expertise in immunoassay technologies (such as Immuno assay PCR, or digital ELISA), assay validation and verification.

 - Strong knowledge of laboratory quality systems, proficiency testing, and inspection readiness.

 - Experience with LIMS platforms, electronic quality management systems (eQMS), and workflow automation in a high-complexity testing environment.

 - Experience working for a fast-paced, early-stage startup.



We offer competitiv

source_jobs.title: Scientist/Senior Scientist, Assay Development - Neuroscience Biomarkers
source_jobs.description:
Spear Bio, founded in 2021, is a well-funded and rapidly growing biotech start-up headquartered in Woburn, Massachusetts.  Our proprietary technology, Successive Proximity Extension Amplification Reaction (SPEAR), is a novel wash-free immunoassay platform, offering unprecedented sensitivity. We are dedicated to revolutionizing early disease diagnosis and monitoring by providing scalable, ultrasensitive solutions that deliver unparalleled accuracy in measuring low-abundant biomarkers, enabling clinicians and researchers to improve patient outcomes and advance global health.


SCIENTIST/SENIOR SCIENTIST, ASSAY DEVELOPMENT - NEUROSCIENCE BIOMARKERS:

Our Assay Development team translates emerging biomarker science into robust assays for research and future clinical applications. We are seeking a Scientist / Senior Scientist who is intellectually curious, hands-on, self-motivated, and comfortable solving difficult scientific problems to play a pivotal role in advancing initiatives for our new range of ultrasensitive immunoassays. The ideal candidate combines deep neurobiology knowledge with experimental rigor, practical troubleshooting skills, and a strong sense of accountability.

The successful candidate will help optimize assay strategies, identify the most relevant molecular forms and epitopes, evaluate preanalytical variables and matrix effects, interpret clinical-sample data, and troubleshoot complex assay-development challenges.

We welcome applications from exceptional recent Ph.D. graduates and postdoctoral fellows who have demonstrated strong scientific ownership, as well as industry scientists who have rapidly taken on increasing responsibility in biotechnology or diagnostics environments. **This role is based onsite in our lab in Woburn, MA 5 days/week.




RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Develop a portfolio of ultrasensitive immunoassays throughout the product lifecycle, from initial screening and optimization through feasibility, verification, and transfer.

 - Plan, design, and execute hands-on assay-development studies, including antibody selection and pairing, reagent conjugation, buffer optimization, reference-material evaluation, matrix characterization, and analytical validation for research and future clinical applications.

 - Provide scientific leadership and translate biomarker biology into assay-design strategies by optimizing for clinically relevant molecular forms, isoforms, post-translational modifications, cleavage fragments, aggregation states, epitopes, and sample types.

 - Analyze data from clinical and preclinical samples and interpret findings in the context of disease biology, sample characteristics, and published literature.

 - Collaborate closely with Platform, Product Development, Operations, and Business Development teams to transition assays from feasibility through verification and transfer.

 - Prepare clear scientific presentations, technical reports, study summaries, abstracts, and publications.

 - Contribute to a rigorous, accountable, and highly collaborative startup environment.




REQUIREMENTS:

 - Ph.D. in Neuroscience, Neurobiology, Molecular Biology, Biochemistry, Biomedical Engineering, or a closely related field.

 - Strong scientific expertise in fluid biomarkers and assay-development strategies for the diagnosis, stratification, or therapeutic monitoring of neurodegenerative diseases, such as Alzheimer’s disease, Parkinson’s disease, or related neurological disorders.

 - Strong understanding of biomarker heterogeneity and the factors that influence measurement in human biofluids.

 - Hands-on experience developing immunoassays or other quantitative protein-detection methods.

 - Demonstrated ability to design experiments independently and troubleshoot technical challenges.

 - Evidence of meaningful scientific contributions through peer-reviewed publications, patents, conference presentations, or translational research programs.

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills.

source_jobs.title: Technician, Bioinstrumentation Systems
source_jobs.description:
Spear Bio, founded in 2021, is a well-funded and rapidly growing biotech start-up headquartered in Woburn, Massachusetts.  Our proprietary technology, Successive Proximity Extension Amplification Reaction (SPEAR), is a novel wash-free immunoassay platform, offering unprecedented sensitivity. We are dedicated to revolutionizing early disease diagnosis and monitoring by providing scalable, ultrasensitive solutions that deliver unparalleled accuracy in measuring low-abundant biomarkers, enabling clinicians and researchers to improve patient outcomes and advance global health.


TECHNICIAN, INSTRUMENTATION SYSTEMS

We are seeking an Instrumentation Systems Technician to support the manufacturing, quality, sustaining, and field service operations for Spear Bio’s new instrument platform. This hands-on role is central to ensuring that every instrument leaving our facility meets rigorous quality standards, and that deployed instruments remain in peak operating condition throughout their lifecycle. You will work at the intersection of hardware assembly, system verification, preventive maintenance, and over time will get involved in customer-facing field support.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:




INSTRUMENT INTAKE, QC & OUTBOUND

• Perform incoming inspection and intake QC of instrument manufacturing units received from CDMO and third-party suppliers, verifying mechanical, electrical, and cosmetic acceptance criteria.

• Execute standardized IQ/OQ verification protocols on each instrument prior to shipment, including functional testing of liquid handling (FAST unit), thermal modules, optics, and motion subsystems.

• Prepare instruments for outbound shipment including final configuration, packaging, labeling, and documentation (traveler sign-off, serial number tracking, shipping checklists).

• Identify and disposition of non-conforming units; document findings and escalate to engineering for root cause investigation.




IN-HOUSE SUSTAINING & PREVENTIVE MAINTENANCE

• Perform scheduled preventive maintenance (PM) on in-house instrument systems and FAST instruments per established service protocols.

• Troubleshoot and repair instrumentation issues across mechanical, electrical, fluidic, and optical subsystems.

• Maintain instrument service records, PM logs, and spare parts inventory.

• Support engineering change order (ECO) implementation on existing instruments, including retrofit kits and firmware/software updates.




FIELD SERVICE & CUSTOMER SUPPORT

• Perform occasional on-site instrument installations (IQ/OQ), preventive maintenance visits, and troubleshooting at customer sites as the field service function scales.

• Serve as a technical point of contact during customer escalations; diagnose issues remotely where possible and dispatch for on-site resolution when needed.

• Document field service activities including installation reports, service tickets, and lessons learned for continuous improvement.

• Travel to customer sites within the U.S. as needed (estimated 10–25% travel, increasing as the installed base grows).




HARDWARE, SOFTWARE & WORKFLOW TESTING SUPPORT

• Assist engineering with system-level and subsystem-level testing of hardware, software, and automated assay workflows as needed.

• Execute test protocols and document results for verification and validation activities.

• Provide hands-on support for new product introduction (NPI) builds and prototype bring-up.

• Contribute to test fixture design, calibration procedures, and tooling improvements.




QUALIFICATIONS:




EDUCATION & EXPERIENCE

• Associate’s degree or technical diploma in Biomedical Equipment Technology, Electronics, Mechatronics, Mechanical Technology, or related field. Bachelor’s degree is a plus but not required.

• 3+ years of hands-on experience in instrument assembly, test, service, or manufacturing in a life science, diagnostics, or analytical instrumentation environment.

• Experience with IQ/OQ/PQ protocols or regulated manufacturing env

source_jobs.title: Business Development Manager, Immunoassays & Biomarkers (Northeast)
source_jobs.description:
Spear Bio, founded in 2021, is a well-funded and rapidly growing biotech start-up headquartered in Woburn, Massachusetts.  Our proprietary technology, Successive Proximity Extension Amplification Reaction (SPEAR), is a novel wash-free immunoassay platform, offering unprecedented sensitivity. We are dedicated to revolutionizing early disease diagnosis and monitoring by providing scalable, ultrasensitive solutions that deliver unparalleled accuracy in measuring low-abundant biomarkers, enabling clinicians and researchers to improve patient outcomes and advance global health.

Position Summary:

Spear Bio is seeking top-performing Business Development Managers to introduce our high-performance and expanding portfolio of ultra-sensitive Immunoassays to the Life Sciences and CRO markets. We are hiring individuals to cover the Northeast (New York, Connecticut, Rhode Island, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, Vermont, Maine) with a deep network of neurodegenerative disease research customers in Big Pharma, Biotech, CROs, & Academia throughout this region.



Key Responsibilities:

 - Deliver sales presentations to clients to promote the value proposition of Spear Bio.

 - Demonstrate deep understanding of Neuroscience market dynamics and technology adoption which positions Spear Bio as a superior, best-in-class solution.

 - Promote our immunoassay kit sales by providing various instrument and Assay Service solutions to generate ongoing market adoption and revenue growth.

 - Provide a funnel to be managed by senior Business Development Managers

 - Be on-point for all customer logistics and follow up: Demos, information sharing, and other key technical details requested to engage our customers.

 - Utilize your existing network of high-level scientific customers within Big Pharma, Biotech, CROs, & Academic market segments, as well as knowledge of IVD markets including LDTs.

 - Achieve a consistent track record exceeding quotas, revenue targets, and increasing market share in your primary territory/region.

 - Provide technical support to many of our Key Opinion Leaders throughout the US/EMEA.

 - Work closely with our internal Marketing team and contribute to driving lead generation programs that can enhance our outreach in all regions.

 - Work closely with our Assay Services leader to support funnel building of this area, as well as provide Voice of Customer (VOC) feedback to our R&D teams on market dynamics gained with our commercial activity.

 - Jointly build a sales plan with personal and professional goals that will provide the best metrics for success in the role.

 - Provide regular updates on activity, forecasting, and funnel movement to assist in the transparency of activity driving strong revenue outcomes.

 - Apply insight on current competitive landscape to develop strategies enabling Spear Bio to win in a competitive market where we have a strong scientific advantage.

 - Approximately 30-40% travel expected directly with customers and/or partners to achieve the sales results expected.



Required Qualifications:

 - MS or PhD in Biology, Biochemistry, Bioengineering or related field with 4-10+ years of an established track record in sales or business development, presenting ultrasensitive immunoassays or neurology IVD products to customers.

 - Clear and pro-active communicator with advanced commercial sales acumen, high energy, a strong work ethic, and a bias for action.

 - Ability to ask the right questions of potential customers to then strategically communicate how Spear Bio's differentiated technology and performance will directly benefit them over the competition.

 - Ability to clearly present technology solutions in a way that leads to strong revenue outcomes.

 - Ability to translate observations and customer feedback into strategy.

 - Comfortable providing and receiving feedback designed to improve engagement and results.

 - Ability to collaborate within a best-in-class team environment comprised of Field Ap
